title: Equal COLA Act
plain_summary: This bill changes how much extra money retired federal workers get each year to help with rising costs. Right now, workers in the Federal Employees Retirement System get less money than the full amount that prices go up. The bill would give these workers the same full cost of living increases that workers in the older Civil Service Retirement System already get. This means their retirement payments would go up by the same amount that things like food and gas get more expensive each year.
last_action_text: ASSUMING FIRST SPONSORSHIP - Mr. Walkinshaw asked unanimous consent that he may hereafter be considered as the first sponsor of H.R. 491, a bill originally introduced by Representative Connolly, for t
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Equal COLA Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Increase in COLA for FERS Annuities
sections.text: This section changes how cost of living adjustments work for federal workers in the Federal Employees Retirement System. Starting on December first of each year, retirement payments from this system will go up by the same amount that prices have gone up. The increase will be based on comparing prices from the current year to prices from the previous year when an adjustment was last made. The increase will be rounded to the nearest one tenth of one percent. This new rule applies to all cost of living adjustments made after this law is passed. It also applies to all retirement payments under this system, whether they started before, on, or after this law takes effect.
